message:
fix(remote): inline TTL expiry in IdempotencyStore::check()

IdempotencyStore::check() now accepts a `now: Time` parameter and
evicts expired records inline during lookup, failing closed to New
instead of returning stale Duplicate decisions. Previously, callers
had to remember a separate evict_expired() pass before deduplication;
if that step was missed, expired keys would linger as stale duplicates.

The signature change from check(&self, ...) to check(&mut self, ..., now)
propagates to all call sites: SimNode::handle_spawn_request passes virtual
time, and all unit/integration tests pass explicit timestamps.

Updated tests: idempotency_store_duplicate_returns_cached_outcome,
idempotency_store_conflict_detection, and all IdempotencyStore unit tests
now supply explicit Time values. SimNode harness comment updated to
clarify that both evict_expired() (bulk hygiene) and check() (inline
per-key) work together for complete TTL enforcement.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
